message:
feat(brenner-loop): add session-hypothesis containment model

Implements the session-hypothesis relationship model:
- Sessions contain multiple hypotheses (one-to-many)
- Hypotheses can be: primary, alternative, or archived
- Competition resolution with winner/loser tracking
- Evolution graph queries (ancestors, descendants, common ancestor)

New functions:
- getSessionHypotheses, getAllHypothesisIds, getHypothesisState
- getActiveHypotheses, hasThirdAlternative, getHypothesisCounts
- setPrimaryHypothesis, addCompetingHypothesis
- resolveCompetition, archiveHypothesis, restoreHypothesis
- getRelatedHypotheses, getEvolutionChain, findCommonAncestor

brenner_bot-0lff

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"brenner_bot-0fs","title":"Task: Create GitHub Actions workflow for tests","description":"Create CI workflow to run tests on every PR.

## Workflow Configuration
- Trigger on pull_request and push to main
- Run unit tests with Vitest
- Run E2E tests with Playwright
- Report coverage
- Cache dependencies

## Steps
1. Checkout code
2. Setup Bun
3. Install dependencies
4. Run lint
5. Run type-check
6. Run unit tests
7. Run E2E tests
8. Upload coverage report
9. Upload test artifacts

## Requirements
- Fast feedback (target: <5 minutes for unit tests)
- Clear failure messages
- Artifacts preservation for debugging","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:12:15.780691552-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T23:42:52.472532836-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T23:42:52.472532836-05:00","close_reason":"Created .github/workflows/test.yml with lint, unit tests, build, E2E, typecheck, and CLI test jobs","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-0fs","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-djz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:12:31.048935463-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-0fs","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-0b5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:12:31.150541808-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-0fs","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-xfs","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:12:31.183709782-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-0kp","title":"Create first-time user onboarding flow","description":"# Task

Design and implement an onboarding experience for first-time visitors.

# The Problem

Users arriving at brennerbot.org need to quickly understand:
1. What is this? (A research tool based on Sydney Brenner's method)
2. Who was Brenner? (Brief bio)
3. Why does this matter? (Value proposition)
4. What can I do here? (Key actions)

# Onboarding Elements

## 1. Hero Section (already planned)
- Animated quotes give a taste of Brenner's voice
- Clear value proposition

## 2. 'What Is This?' Section (NEW)
A brief, scannable explanation:

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  WHAT IS BRENNER BOT?                                        │
│                                                              │
│  Sydney Brenner (1927-2019) was one of the most successful  │
│  experimental biologists in history. But his METHOD is more  │
│  valuable than any single discovery.                         │
│                                                              │
│  This project reverse-engineers his cognitive architecture   │
│  and makes it executable for AI-assisted research.           │
│                                                              │
│  [📖 Read the Corpus]  [🔬 Explore the Method]               │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## 3. Quick Tour Option
- 'Take a quick tour' button
- Step-through overlay highlighting key sections
- Can be dismissed / 'Don't show again'

## 4. Guided Entry Points
Instead of just 'Browse Corpus', offer guided paths:
- 'New to Brenner? Start here →' (curated intro excerpts)
- 'Compare AI syntheses →' (distillations comparison)
- 'Ready to research? Start a session →'

# Implementation

## Tour Library
Use something like `react-joyride` or `intro.js` for the guided tour.

## Persistence
Store 'tour completed' in localStorage to not show again.

## Mobile
Simpler onboarding on mobile - maybe just the 'What Is This?' section without overlay tour.

# Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] 'What Is This?' section explains the project
- [ ] Optional tour available for new users
- [ ] Guided entry points help users choose their path
- [ ] Tour state persists (don't show again)
- [ ] Works on mobile","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RedHill","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:22:34.53227297-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T13:49:53.182058057-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T13:49:53.182058057-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented WhatIsThis onboarding section with guided entry points and localStorage persistence","labels":["onboarding","ux","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-0kp","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-9o2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:23:06.972337943-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-0kp","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-hv6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:23:07.002380154-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-0kp","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-ggv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:27:49.010916091-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
-{"id":"brenner_bot-0lff","title":"TASK: Define Session-Hypothesis Containment Model","description":"
## Clarifying the Relationship

A Brenner Loop SESSION contains one or more HYPOTHESES. But this relationship isn't explicit in our data models.

### The Questions
1. Can a session have multiple active hypotheses?
2. Can a hypothesis belong to multiple sessions?
3. How do competing hypotheses within a session relate?
4. What happens to hypotheses when session closes?

### Proposed Model

#### One-to-Many: Session → Hypotheses
```typescript
interface Session {
  id: string;
  // ... other fields
  
  // A session contains multiple hypotheses
  hypotheses: {
    primary: string;      // The main hypothesis ID
    alternatives: string[]; // Competing hypothesis IDs
    archived: string[];   // Discarded/superseded hypothesis IDs
  };
}

interface HypothesisCard {
  id: string;
  sessionId: string;  // Belongs to exactly one session
  
  // Relationship to other hypotheses in same session
  relationship?: {
    type: 'alternative_to' | 'refinement_of' | 'supersedes';
    relatedId: string;
  };
}
```

#### Session-Hypothesis Lifecycle
```
Session Created
     │
     ▼
┌─────────────────┐
│ Primary Hypo    │ ◄─── Initial hypothesis
└────────┬────────┘
         │
         ▼ (user adds competing hypothesis)
┌─────────────────┐     ┌─────────────────┐
│ Primary Hypo    │ ◄──►│ Alternative 1   │
└────────┬────────┘     └────────┬────────┘
         │                       │
         └───────────┬───────────┘
                     │
                     ▼ (testing eliminates Alternative 1)
             ┌─────────────────┐
             │ Primary Hypo    │ ◄─── Winner
             │ (refined)       │
             └────────┬────────┘
                      │
                      ▼
              Session Synthesis
```

### Key Behaviors

#### Adding Competing Hypothesis
```typescript
async function addCompetingHypothesis(
  sessionId: string,
  competingWith: string,
  newHypothesis: Partial<HypothesisCard>
): Promise<HypothesisCard> {
  // Create new hypothesis linked to session
  const hypo = await createHypothesis({
    ...newHypothesis,
    sessionId,
    relationship: {
      type: 'alternative_to',
      relatedId: competingWith,
    }
  });
  
  // Update session to track alternative
  await updateSession(sessionId, {
    hypotheses: {
      ...session.hypotheses,
      alternatives: [...session.hypotheses.alternatives, hypo.id]
    }
  });
  
  return hypo;
}
```

#### Resolving Competition
```typescript
async function resolveCompetition(
  sessionId: string,
  winnerId: string,
  loserId: string,
  reason: string
): Promise<void> {
  // Archive the loser
  await updateHypothesis(loserId, {
    state: 'superseded',
    supersededBy: winnerId,
    supersededReason: reason,
  });
  
  // Update session
  await updateSession(sessionId, {
    hypotheses: {
      primary: winnerId,
      alternatives: session.hypotheses.alternatives.filter(id => id !== loserId),
      archived: [...session.hypotheses.archived, loserId]
    }
  });
}
```

### File Location
`apps/web/src/lib/brenner-loop/sessionHypothesis.ts`

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Session data model includes hypothesis relationships
- [ ] Can add competing hypotheses to session
- [ ] Can resolve competition (winner/loser)
- [ ] Archived hypotheses preserved for learning

### Depends On
- 1v26.1 (Session Data Model)
- an1n.1 (HypothesisCard Interface)

```typescript
  return hypo;
```

```typescript
  await updateSession(sessionId, {
    hypotheses: {
      primary: winnerId,
      alternatives: session.hypotheses.alternatives.filter(id => id !== loserId),
      archived: [...session.hypotheses.archived, loserId]
    }

}
```

### File Location
`apps/web/src/lib/brenner-loop/sessionHypothesis.ts`

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Session data model includes hypothesis relationships
- [ ] Can add competing hypotheses to session
- [ ] Can resolve competition (winner/loser)
- [ ] Archived hypotheses preserved for learning

- an1n.1 (HypothesisCard Interface)

 {"id":"brenner_bot-0li","title":"Create distillations overview page with model cards","description":"# Task

Build the /distillations overview page that introduces all three model syntheses.

# Page Structure

1. **Header** - 'The Three Distillations' with brief intro
2. **Model Cards** - One card per model (Opus, GPT, Gemini)
3. **Comparison CTA** - Link to comparison view

# Model Card Content

Each card shows:
- Model name + logo/icon
- Signature color (Opus: orange, GPT: blue, Gemini: cyan)
- Metaphor ('Philosophy of Science' / 'Decision Theory' / 'Operating System')
- Core question it answers
- 2-3 unique contributions
- 'Read Full' button

# Layout

- 3-column grid on desktop
- Single column on mobile
- Cards have equal height

# Why This First

Users need an entry point to understand the distillations before diving into comparison. This page provides that orientation.

# Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Three model cards render correctly
- [ ] Each card has correct content
- [ ] Links to individual distillation pages work
- [ ] Comparison CTA visible
- [ ] Responsive layout","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:20:22.627743856-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T21:32:20.05337566-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T21:32:20.05337566-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented: /distillations page has model cards, comparison table, convergences section, reading guide, responsive layout","labels":["distillations","overview","ux","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-0li","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-8yn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:21:00.256561797-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-0li","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-igw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:25:02.93339356-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-0li","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.4.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:25:28.455279492-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-0li","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.4.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:25:28.508908397-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"brenner_bot-0meb","title":"Task: Core Library Unit Tests (No Mocks)","description":"# Goal
Add unit tests for all untested library modules in `apps/web/src/lib/`.

# Modules to Test

## 1. corpus.ts (8.2KB)
Test corpus configuration and data loading:
- Document metadata retrieval
- Category filtering
- Document path resolution
- Corpus index generation

## 2. prompt-builder.ts (7.8KB)
Test prompt composition:
- Template variable substitution
- Excerpt injection
- Role prompt assembly
- Token counting/truncation

## 3. prompts.ts (2.2KB)
Test prompt templates:
- Template string validity
- Variable placeholder presence
- Default value handling

## 4. search/engine.ts
Test search engine core:
- Query parsing
- Index building
- Result ranking
- Fuzzy matching

## 5. search/hooks.ts
Test search React hooks:
- useSearch state management
- Debounced search
- Result caching
- Error state handling

## 6. operators.ts + operators.local.ts
Test operator definitions:
- Operator lookup by symbol
- Category filtering
- Description retrieval
- Local operator overrides

# Test Pattern
```typescript
// corpus.test.ts
import { getCorpusDocument, getCorpusIndex } from './corpus';

describe('corpus', () => {
  describe('getCorpusDocument', () => {
    it('returns document metadata for valid doc ID', () => {
      const doc = getCorpusDocument('transcript-batch-1');
      expect(doc).toMatchObject({
        id: 'transcript-batch-1',
        type: 'transcript',
        title: expect.any(String),
      });
    });
    
    it('returns null for unknown doc ID', () => {
      expect(getCorpusDocument('nonexistent')).toBeNull();
    });
  });
});
```

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] corpus.test.ts with ≥80% coverage
- [ ] prompt-builder.test.ts with ≥80% coverage
- [ ] prompts.test.ts with 100% coverage (small file)
- [ ] search/engine.test.ts with ≥80% coverage
- [ ] search/hooks.test.ts with ≥80% coverage
- [ ] operators.test.ts with ≥80% coverage
- [ ] All tests use real corpus data, no mock documents","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PinkLake","created_at":"2025-12-30T17:50:42.137578449-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-31T00:17:03.729511182-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-31T00:17:03.729511182-05:00","close_reason":"All 5 core library test files created/verified: prompts.test.ts, corpus.test.ts, prompt-builder.test.ts, search/engine.test.ts, search/hooks.test.ts. 1370 tests pass.","labels":["testing unit-tests lib"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-0meb","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-8dy3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T22:36:16.082494539-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"brenner_bot-y6z","title":"Create BrennerQuote component with beautiful styling","description":"# Task

Create a stunning quote component specifically for Brenner quotes.

# Why Special Treatment

Brenner's quotes are the crown jewels of this project. They deserve more than gray italic text. Each quote should feel like wisdom worth savoring.

# Design

```
┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  ❝                                                      │
│    Exclusion is always a tremendously good              │
│    thing in science.                                    │
│                                                         │
│                           — Sydney Brenner, §42         │
│                                      [📋] [🔗]          │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

# Visual Elements

1. **Opening quote mark** - Large, decorative, in primary color (faded)
2. **Quote text** - Larger font, good line height, slightly italic
3. **Gradient border** - Left border with primary→accent gradient
4. **Background** - Subtle card background with glassmorphism
5. **Attribution** - Right-aligned, smaller, with § link
6. **Action buttons** - Copy, Share (appear on hover)

# Animation

- Fade + slide in on scroll reveal
- Subtle glow on hover
- Button reveal on hover

# Props

```tsx
interface BrennerQuoteProps {
  children: React.ReactNode;
  source?: string;        // e.g., '§42' or 'Interview, 1998'
  sourceUrl?: string;     // Link to transcript section
  variant?: 'default' | 'featured' | 'inline';
}
```

# Variants

- **default** - Standard quote block
- **featured** - Larger, more prominent (for landing page)
- **inline** - Compact, for use within paragraphs

# Accessibility

- Proper `<blockquote>` semantics
- `<cite>` for attribution
- Focus states on buttons
- Screen reader announces as quote

# Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Quote renders with all visual elements
- [ ] Animation on scroll reveal
- [ ] Copy button works
- [ ] Share button opens share dialog
- [ ] § link navigates correctly
- [ ] All three variants work
- [ ] Accessible markup","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:21:53.472975562-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T19:29:11.423890236-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T19:29:11.423890236-05:00","close_reason":"Folded into landing quote presentation (brenner_bot-9o2)","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-y6z","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-8yn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:23:06.812013867-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-y6z","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-2n1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:23:06.847436402-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-y8pv","title":"Fix threadStatus pending ACK logic for implicit replies","description":"ThreadStatus marks pending ACKs by scanning for ACK messages, but Agent Mail acknowledgements are not thread messages. Treat any post-kickoff reply as implicit acknowledgement and include bcc recipients.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-02T09:07:45.549866186-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-02T09:08:03.68191858-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-02T09:08:03.68191858-05:00","close_reason":"Updated threadStatus pending-ack logic to treat post-kickoff replies as implicit acks (plus bcc) and added regression test."}
-{"id":"brenner_bot-y8px","title":"TASK: Build Hypothesis Comparison View","description":"
## Side-by-Side Hypothesis Comparison

When users have competing hypotheses (via Competing Hypotheses Arena - an1n.6), they need a clear way to compare them.

### Comparison View Design

\`\`\`
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Compare Hypotheses                              [Exit Compare] │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                 │
│  Hypothesis A                    vs    Hypothesis B            │
│  ────────────────                      ────────────────         │
│                                                                 │
│  Statement:                            Statement:               │
│  "Social media causes                  "Pre-existing anxiety    │
│  anxiety through                       leads to increased       │
│  comparison mechanisms"                social media use"        │
│                                                                 │
│  Mechanism:                            Mechanism:               │
│  Upward social comparison              Anxiety → seeking        │
│  triggers inadequacy                   reassurance online       │
│                                                                 │
│  Key Prediction:                       Key Prediction:          │
│  Reducing SM time                      Treating anxiety will    │
│  decreases anxiety                     reduce SM usage          │
│                                                                 │
│  Confidence: 72%                       Confidence: 45%          │
│  ████████████░░░░                      ████████░░░░░░░░         │
│                                                                 │
│  ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── │
│                                                                 │
│  Discriminating Tests:                                          │
│  • Algorithm change → A predicts decrease, B predicts no change │
│  • Clinical treatment → A predicts no change, B predicts effect │
│                                                                 │
│  [Design New Test] [Resolve Competition]                        │
│                                                                 │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
\`\`\`

### Comparison Features

#### 1. Field-by-Field Diff

Highlight differences:
\`\`\`typescript
interface ComparisonResult {
  field: keyof HypothesisCard;
  valueA: string;
  valueB: string;
  similarity: number;  // 0-1
  isConflicting: boolean;
}
\`\`\`

#### 2. Prediction Conflict Matrix

Show where hypotheses make DIFFERENT predictions:
\`\`\`
                    Hypothesis A    Hypothesis B
Test 1 result       supports        challenges
Test 2 result       challenges      supports      ← DISCRIMINATING
Test 3 result       inconclusive    inconclusive
\`\`\`

#### 3. Evidence Summary

Which tests favor which hypothesis:
\`\`\`
Evidence so far:
• 2 tests favor Hypothesis A
• 1 test favors Hypothesis B  
• 1 test inconclusive

Current leader: Hypothesis A (67%)
\`\`\`

#### 4. Resolution Actions

When competition is resolved:
- Declare winner
- Merge insights from loser
- Archive loser (with learning notes)
- Generate comparison section in Research Brief

### Entry Points

1. From Competing Hypotheses Arena
2. From session dashboard (when multiple hypos)
3. From "Compare" button on hypothesis card

### File Locations
\`\`\`
apps/web/src/components/brenner-loop/ComparisonView.tsx
apps/web/src/components/brenner-loop/HypothesisDiff.tsx
apps/web/src/components/brenner-loop/PredictionMatrix.tsx
apps/web/src/lib/brenner-loop/comparison.ts
\`\`\`

- [ ] Differences highlighted
- [ ] Prediction conflicts shown
- [ ] Evidence summary displayed
- [ ] Resolution flow works

 {"id":"brenner_bot-yh1c","title":"TASK: Create Operator Documentation & In-Session Help","description":"
## What Do These Operators Actually Mean?

Users encounter operator names like "Level Split (Σ)" but may not understand:
- What the operator does conceptually
- When to apply it
- How to fill out the form
- What a good output looks like

### Documentation for Each Operator

#### Level Split (Σ)
- **Concept**: Identify different levels of explanation (molecular, cellular, organism, population)
- **When to use**: When your hypothesis conflates multiple levels
- **Key question**: "At what level is this phenomenon happening?"
- **Example**: Brenner splitting genetic coding from protein folding
- **Form guidance**: "List 2-4 distinct levels that could explain your observation"

#### Exclusion Test (⊘)
- **Concept**: Design a test that could EXCLUDE your hypothesis
- **When to use**: When you need to discriminate between possibilities
- **Key question**: "What result would prove you wrong?"
- **Example**: Brenner's triplet exclusion experiments
- **Form guidance**: "Describe what you would observe if your hypothesis is FALSE"

#### Object Transpose (⟳)
- **Concept**: Look at the problem from a different angle or reference frame
- **When to use**: When stuck or missing something obvious
- **Key question**: "What if I inverted the relationship?"
- **Example**: Brenner asking "what DOESN'T the gene do?"
- **Form guidance**: "Restate your hypothesis from the opposite perspective"

#### Scale Check (⊙)
- **Concept**: Verify the hypothesis works at different scales
- **When to use**: When effect might be scale-dependent
- **Key question**: "Does this hold at 10x or 0.1x scale?"
- **Example**: Brenner checking if molecular mechanism works at organism level
- **Form guidance**: "Describe how you'd test at different magnitudes"

### In-Session Help Integration

\`\`\`
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Apply: Level Split (Σ)                               [?] Help │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                 │
│  "Before you can test anything, you need to know what          │
│  LEVEL you're operating at."                                   │
│                                     — Sydney Brenner            │
│                                                                 │
│  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  │
│                                                                 │
│  Your hypothesis: [Social media causes anxiety in teens]       │
│                                                                 │
│  What levels of explanation might apply?                        │
│                                                                 │
│  Level 1: [Neurochemical - dopamine/cortisol]                  │
│  Level 2: [Psychological - comparison/self-esteem]             │
│  Level 3: [Social - peer dynamics]                             │
│  Level 4: [                                              ]      │
│                                                                 │
│  💡 Tip: Try to span at least 2 different scales               │
│                                                                 │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
\`\`\`

### Contextual Help Panel

Click [?] to expand:
- What is Level Split?
- When should I use it?
- See example from Brenner
- Watch 2-min video (future)

### File Locations
\`\`\`
apps/web/src/lib/brenner-loop/operators/docs.ts       → Documentation content
apps/web/src/components/brenner-loop/OperatorHelp.tsx → Help component
apps/web/src/components/brenner-loop/OperatorTip.tsx  → Inline tips
\`\`\`

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Each operator has written documentation
- [ ] Help button available in operator UI
- [ ] Contextual tips in forms
- [ ] Example from Brenner shown
- [ ] Links to relevant corpus sections

### Depends On
- vw6p.1 (Operator Session Framework)

 {"id":"brenner_bot-yh8l","title":"Task: Implement Scoring Logic for All Dimensions","description":"# Task: Implement Scoring Logic for All Dimensions

## Objective
Implement the scoring logic for all 7 Brenner method dimensions as defined in evaluation_rubric_v0.1.md.

## Implementation Details

### File Location
`apps/web/src/lib/brenner-scorecard.ts`

### Core Types
```typescript
export interface DimensionScore {
  dimension: string;
  points: number;
  maxPoints: number;
  percentage: number;
  signals: ScoreSignal[];
}

export interface ScoreSignal {
  signal: string;
  points: number;
  found: boolean;
  evidence?: string;
}

export interface SessionScore {
  sessionId: string;
  scoredAt: string;
  dimensions: {
    paradoxGrounding: DimensionScore;
    hypothesisKillRate: DimensionScore;
    testDiscriminability: DimensionScore;
    assumptionTracking: DimensionScore;
    thirdAlternativeDiscovery: DimensionScore;
    experimentalFeasibility: DimensionScore;
    adversarialPressure: DimensionScore;
  };
  totalScore: number;
  maxScore: number;
  grade: 'A' | 'B' | 'C' | 'D' | 'F';
}
```

### Dimension Scoring Functions

#### 1. Paradox Grounding (0-20)
```typescript
export function scoreParadoxGrounding(session: SessionData): DimensionScore {
  const signals: ScoreSignal[] = [
    {
      signal: 'Research question contains explicit paradox/puzzle',
      points: 5,
      found: checkForParadoxKeywords(session.researchQuestion),
    },
    {
      signal: 'At least one surprising observation cited',
      points: 5,
      found: session.artifact.anomalyRegister.length > 0,
    },
    {
      signal: 'Question challenges existing paradigm',
      points: 5,
      found: checkForParadigmChallenge(session.researchQuestion),
    },
    {
      signal: 'Foundational assumptions questioned',
      points: 5,
      found: session.artifact.assumptionLedger.some(a => a.challenged),
    },
  ];
  
  return computeDimensionScore('paradoxGrounding', signals, 20);
}
```

#### 2. Hypothesis Kill Rate (0-20)
```typescript
export function scoreHypothesisKillRate(session: SessionData): DimensionScore {
  const transitions = getSessionTransitions(session.id);
  const kills = transitions.filter(t => t.toState === 'killed');
  
  const signals: ScoreSignal[] = [
    {
      signal: 'At least one hypothesis killed in session',
      points: 10,
      found: kills.length > 0,
      evidence: kills.length > 0 ? `${kills.length} hypotheses killed` : undefined,
    },
    {
      signal: 'Kill linked to specific test result',
      points: 5,
      found: kills.some(k => k.triggeredBy?.startsWith('T-')),
    },
    {
      signal: 'Kill reasoning documented',
      points: 5,
      found: kills.every(k => k.reason && k.reason.length > 10),
    },
  ];
  
  // Penalty: No hypotheses transitioned at all
  if (transitions.length === 0) {
    signals.push({
      signal: 'No hypothesis transitions (static session)',
      points: -5,
      found: true,
    });
  }
  
  return computeDimensionScore('hypothesisKillRate', signals, 20);
}
```

#### 3-7: Similar pattern for remaining dimensions
- testDiscriminability: Check predictions table, mutual exclusivity
- assumptionTracking: Check assumption ledger, linked hypotheses
- thirdAlternativeDiscovery: Check for third_alternative origin hypotheses
- experimentalFeasibility: Check methodology, resource fields
- adversarialPressure: Check adversarial critique section

### Grade Calculation
```typescript
export function computeGrade(totalScore: number): 'A' | 'B' | 'C' | 'D' | 'F' {
  if (totalScore >= 90) return 'A';
  if (totalScore >= 80) return 'B';
  if (totalScore >= 70) return 'C';
  if (totalScore >= 60) return 'D';
  return 'F';
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 7 dimensions have scoring functions
- [ ] Each signal contributes documented points
- [ ] Negative signals (penalties) supported
- [ ] Grade computed from total score
- [ ] Evidence captured for audit

## Dependencies
- Feature: Hypothesis Lifecycle States (for transition data)
- Feature: Test→Hypothesis Binding (for test data)

## Effort Estimate
~300 lines of code, ~4 hours","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:49:04.056579492-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-31T03:05:42.261946831-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-31T03:05:42.261946831-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented 7-dimension session scoring: paradoxGrounding, hypothesisKillRate, testDiscriminability, assumptionTracking, thirdAlternativeDiscovery, experimentalFeasibility, adversarialPressure. Added 32 unit tests. All 81 scorecard tests pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-yh8l","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-nihv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:50:56.641751924-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-yh8l","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-6n4e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:50:56.805572663-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-yh8l","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-m6yw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:50:56.835529332-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-yh8l","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-jcuz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T22:41:49.100177647-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
